content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To: Ken
Date: 1-21-77

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Dawn

Subject: Subject - Opinion Request Meeting

Is interest income received from a bankers acceptance note reportable as income? Yes

A bankers acceptance note is usually a short term investment similar to a treasury note. The investment may be withdrawn at any time and would be sold on the open market which may result in a loss or profit if the discount rate, at the time of disposal is at a different rate than the rate at the time of purchase.

sections.question: Is interest income received from a bankers acceptance note reportable as income?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether interest income from a bankers acceptance note is reportable as required income.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Interest income received from a bankers acceptance note is reportable as income. A bankers acceptance note is a short-term investment similar to treasury notes that can be withdrawn and sold on the open market, with potential gains or losses based on discount rate changes.
